source.organizationName: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

content: Taking medications correctly is one of the most important things you can do for your health. Here's what you need to know: Why medication adherence matters: Taking medications as prescribed helps them work properly, prevents your condition from getting worse, and reduces the risk of complications or hospital readmission. Tips for remembering: 1) Use a pill organizer to sort medications by day and time. 2) Set phone alarms or reminders. 3) Link taking medication to daily routines like brushing teeth or meals. 4) Keep a medication list with you at all times. What to include on your medication list: medication name, dose, how often to take it, what it's for, and prescribing doctor. Important safety tips: 1) Never skip doses or stop taking medication without talking to your doctor. 2) Don't share medications with others. 3) Store medications properly - some need refrigeration, others should be kept dry. 4) Check expiration dates regularly. 5) Keep medications in original containers with labels. When to call your doctor: If you experience side effects, can't afford your medications, have trouble swallowing pills, or don't understand how to take them. Your pharmacist can also answer questions about your medications.

citationFormat.citationText: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality. (2023). Taking Your Medications Safely. Retrieved from https://www.ahrq.gov/patients-consumers/diagnosis-treatment/treatments/pillcard/pillcard.html
